transcript_text:
spk_0: Um, okay... so, James, tell me a bit about yourself.
spk_0: To begin with, like, what do you do?
spk_0: Are you currently a student?
spk_0: Have you graduated?
spk_0: What do you work at, if so, etc.?
spk_1: Yeah, no worries. I live in Bristol...
spk_1: live and work in Bristol.
spk_1: I just... I finished a degree at the University of Bristol fairly recently, a couple of months ago.
spk_1: That's a postgraduate degree.
spk_1: I've done a few other degrees too, so I went to Nottingham and UCL and Union of Bristol.
spk_1: This last one was...
spk_1: it was a PhD, which I finished a few months ago.
spk_0: Fantastic, that's great!
spk_0: So, you graduated in, like, September...
spk_0: just past, or...?
spk_1: It was a weird... it was a weird one because it, it's a research degree, so you don't really have a formal, like, intake, you know, standard intake.
spk_1: So it's just basically whenever you get your viva sorted.
spk_1: So I kind of recently passed, but I don't...
spk_1: I don't think... I don't know, I don't officially graduate.
spk_1: Might not have a graduation ceremony for a few months yet, so I'm sort of in a middle period.
spk_0: Nice. Okay, so you're still technically a student, I suppose?
spk_1: Yeah, technically, I'm still registered...
spk_1: yeah.
spk_0: Okay, so you're finished now...
spk_0: you're about to move into full-time work, is that?
spk_1: I'm working already, actually.
spk_1: I was... I was sort of, for some of the PhD, I was taking time off work to do it, and then for some of it, I was working.
spk_1: So, I work as a doctor in...
spk_1: in Bristol, so I'm doing that at the moment.
spk_1: I was sort of doing the latter part of the PhD outside of...
spk_1: in the evenings and weekends.
spk_0: Fantastic! Fair play.
spk_0: Okay, so what does a typical week look like for you?
spk_1: What... what do you mean?
spk_1: Work-wise, or...?
spk_0: Yeah, just in general...
spk_0: like, what does... what does your week look like?
spk_1: Sort of rotating shifts, so every week's kind of different, but this week I'm like on a normal day, so it's just, you know, Monday to Friday...
spk_1: get to work at half seven, leave usually about half five, but today a bit later, and then...
spk_1: Yeah, every day is very different in, in the medical field.
spk_1: So... And then I was doing research and stuff in the evenings and weekends, but now I don't have to, which is nice.
spk_0: Fantastic! Okay, so now you're completely done, and who are you living with at the moment?
spk_1: So, I live... Well, my girlfriend lives nearby, and she's also got a house, but we basically live here, so she's mostly here.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. So, mainly with a partner, and then who...?
spk_0: You mix the decisions around food, then, in your household?
spk_1: So, I kind of decide if we're gonna eat out, have a takeaway, or eat in.
spk_1: And then she decides, like, kind of what we eat...
spk_1: so it's kind of shared.
spk_0: It's a good decision!
spk_1: If we're gonna get takeaway or a meal, I would generally choose, but if we're eating, she'll...
spk_1: she'll pick something.
spk_0: Okay, great! And who's most likely to cook, then?
spk_1: She's more likely to cook...
spk_0: Okay, great! Thank you for that.
spk_0: So, I'm gonna touch base on some of the social media elements...
spk_0: Which social platforms are you currently using?
spk_1: Instagram, Facebook...
spk_1: I watch stuff on YouTube.
spk_1: I don't create content.
spk_1: I've got, like, LinkedIn and stuff.
spk_1: I've got Twitter; I don't really use it anymore, and I'm...
spk_1: I'm not on TikTok because I don't have time.
spk_0: Great! And which of the platforms would you say you use the most?
spk_1: Instagram.
spk_0: If you were then able to order them from your most used to least...
spk_0: what would... what way would you order them?
spk_1: So, I interact mostly on Instagram, and then, I guess, YouTube from a kind of content consumption point of view.
spk_1: And then... and then Facebook.
spk_1: I don't use LinkedIn very much, and I really don't use Twitter anymore.
spk_0: Okay... do you know roughly how much time you spend on social media each day?
spk_1: Too much, as everyone probably does.
spk_1: I would spend, oh, I don't know, very little time on anything other than Instagram, really, and YouTube.
spk_1: I don't know... maybe a couple of hours a day.
spk_1: Maybe less. Yeah...
spk_1: an hour and a half, two hours.
spk_0: Okay, interesting...
spk_0: Thank you! Where do you tend to see the most food-related content online?
spk_1: Instagram, definitely...
spk_1: yeah.
spk_0: Do you follow any food influencers, creators, or local foods?
spk_1: Yeah, there's a couple in Bristol who are really good that I follow, and I can't remember their names...
spk_1: And then there's a couple of restaurants that I like that I follow.
spk_1: There's a couple of chefs...
spk_1: Yeah, that's about it.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. And then, have you ever ordered food because you saw it on social media?
spk_1: Don't think so... Probably have at some point, but it doesn't spring to mind.
spk_0: Okay, thank you for that.
spk_0: So, walk me through, then, the typical week from morning to night.
spk_0: What do you eat, when, and where?
spk_1: Yeah, it depends if I'm working or not and what kind of shift I'm doing—like a long or short shift—but breakfast is usually pretty rushed, to be honest with you.
spk_1: It's kind of... I generally try and make overnight oats and eat it in the car, basically.
spk_1: And then lunch... sometimes I'm organized enough to pre-make stuff or have leftovers.
spk_1: Other times, it's kind of like a meal deal type thing.
spk_1: And then dinner's really varied—anything from eating in, like cooking ourselves, to takeaway, to eating out, to something in between.
spk_1: So that's pretty variable.
spk_1: I'd say in the week, we're more likely to cook ourselves, and in the kind of Friday to Sunday, more likely to eat out or have a takeaway.
spk_0: Great! I was going to ask about the difference between your weekends and weekdays...
spk_0: So, you're more likely to eat out on weekends?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah... unless you've got something, like if we're going to a gig or something, or we might, you know, grab some food before.
spk_1: But yeah, on the whole, we both start pretty early in the week, so we kind of tend to eat in, have an early night, and then do more stuff on the weekends.
spk_0: Fantastic! What do you look for when you're deciding what to eat?
spk_1: Eating in? So we would generally, like, generally try and have healthy stuff if we can...
spk_1: to eat a lot of salmon, lots of, you know, rice and various vegetables and stuff, and then kind of pig out on the weekends and have slightly more unhealthy stuff.
spk_1: So, a combination of how much time we've got and...
spk_1: yeah... whether we're cooking or whether we're gonna eat something.
spk_0: Okay... and how often do you cook?
spk_1: Me personally, or us as a...
spk_1: together. So, I mean, me personally, I guess I would cook once a week, maybe...
spk_1: maybe twice a week.
spk_1: And then my partner would probably cook three or four...
spk_1: three times a week, maybe, I'd say.
spk_1: And then, usually, on average, two times a week, we would have a takeaway or restaurant.
spk_0: Fantastic! And what does cooking mean to you?
spk_1: I... my partner loves cooking, and she's an awesome chef cook.
spk_1: I... I find it... okay.
spk_1: Cooking, to me, doesn't mean a huge amount.
spk_1: I like seeing people enjoy what you've made, but I'm not particularly talented in the kitchen, so it's just like one of those things for me.
spk_0: Interesting. And is it from scratch, or just like pre-prepared?
spk_0: Count is cooking to you...
spk_0: he knows it.
spk_1: When I'm cooking for both of us, it's all from scratch.
spk_1: When it's just myself...
spk_1: she's a whale or something.
spk_1: I sometimes just...
spk_1: lazy and have, like, a...
spk_1: I guess, yeah, ready meal type thing sometimes, or something from a jar or whatever.
spk_1: But yeah, when I'm cooking for other people, it always has to be from scratch.
spk_0: Okay, great. Thank you.
spk_0: And then you touched on ordering food...
spk_0: then twice a week for delivery.
spk_0: What about groceries?
spk_0: How often do you get groceries for delivery?
spk_1: I used to be better at getting, like, a good food shop, and now I've just...
spk_1: it's been really busy recently, so kind of lots of little top-up shops from the local small shop.
spk_1: So, yeah, historically, I would say one to two big food shops a week, and now it's kind of...
spk_1: most days I just grab something, or my partner grabs something.
spk_0: Okay, so... which meals would you say feel the most stressful?
spk_1: I don't think any of them are stressful.
spk_1: Sometimes, just rushing in the morning...
spk_1: breakfast can be a bit less than ideal, like I don't eat as much as I would like to, or have the time to eat it.
spk_1: So, I'd say that's most rushed, but not stressful.
spk_0: Okay... what about—which meals feel the most enjoyable?
spk_1: Definitely dinner...
spk_1: Yeah. And lunch is sometimes rushed as well because you don't have that much time at work.
spk_1: But yeah, definitely dinner.
spk_1: You can have the time to sit and chat and enjoy dinner.
spk_0: So, when does food feel functional, social, and comforting?
spk_1: So, functional's the mornings, definitely.
spk_1: Social is probably lunch...
spk_1: having kind of a sociable lunch at work, and then comforting...
spk_1: I'd say dinner at home or out at a restaurant.
spk_0: Okay, so what about groceries then?
spk_0: We haven't talked about a huge amount yet...
spk_0: So, how often do you typically do grocery shopping in the house, and what's it look like for you?
spk_1: Yeah, so previously I would do one or two food shops a week, like big food shops.
spk_1: Now, I'd say most days I'm ending up just popping into the shops, so now it's kind of just getting as and what I need, and maybe one more substantial shop a week.
spk_1: So I've got quite a few local, kind of, you know, Co-op and Sainsbury's and Tesco's.
spk_1: The little ones... and the big ones are a slightly further drive, so I just get stuff on my way home usually.
spk_0: Great. So, it's mostly top-up shops...
spk_0: Is that fair?
spk_1: Yeah, I think so...
spk_1: mostly these days. Yeah.
spk_0: Do you ever do a big shop?
spk_1: I try and do one a week now, but certainly less than I used to, just because of busyness and things...
spk_0: Sure... Do you know roughly how much you spend on food, as a whole package, in a typical week?
spk_1: No, I don't. I probably should...
spk_1: I really don't know.
spk_1: Every week's so different.
spk_1: It's really hard to say, but...
spk_1: because, you know, if you include restaurants and takeaways...
spk_1: I don't know, a hundred pounds a week, maybe more.
spk_0: And how would that be split, then, across food deliveries, groceries, and eating items?
spk_1: Yeah, most... The majority of that would be when including the restaurants and takeaways, and if—obviously—if they're bigger or more often, then that price goes up.
spk_1: But I don't think the groceries side...
spk_1: I don't think I spend a huge amount a week on.
spk_1: So yeah, maybe two-thirds of that is takeaways, restaurants; one-third groceries, I'd say.
spk_0: Okay... so, what reason do you normally order food for delivery?
spk_1: Delivery, I guess, you know, just...
spk_1: And if we're tired and don't want to cook, or it's a nice special treat, or there's something particular we fancy.
spk_1: I live a bit further out of Bristol, so the restaurants aren't particularly close.
spk_1: Sometimes, it's just easier to get something delivered.
spk_0: Absolutely. Well, walk me through the last time you ordered food for takeaway via one of the apps.
spk_0: Tell me what was happening that day...
spk_0: what triggered it...
spk_0: who were you with...
spk_0: what were you doing...
spk_0: how did you feel...
spk_0: and ultimately, what led to that order?
spk_1: Yeah, I had some sushi as my partner had some sushi on the weekend...
spk_1: not this last week, the weekend before, which app to be used.
spk_1: So I've got the rewards for all, like Uber One, Just Eats...
spk_1: is it Plus? And Delivery Silver, I think it is.
spk_1: So, kind of interchange between them, looking at...
spk_1: depending on what deals are on and what's available.
spk_1: I think that last one, I might have used UberEats because I had some sort of coupon or something...
spk_1: or discount code. But yeah, we were just having sort of a lazy day, watching a film and ratchet around the house, and just got some sushi.
spk_1: Wanted something fairly healthy!
spk_0: Is that the typical type of food you would...
spk_0: you would order into the house, or does it vary?
spk_1: Mix it up? Really, a bit of everything.
spk_1: So, a bit of... sometimes pizza, sometimes curry, sometimes Chinese, sushi...
spk_1: probably quite a lot of Asian dishes and takeaways.
spk_1: Less often a burger or something, but occasionally, a burger.
spk_0: Okay. Are there go-to orders for you?
spk_0: Like, is there a...
spk_0: a go-to restaurant or go-to meal when you think of...?
spk_1: Not really. We try to mix it up, to be honest.
spk_1: We try to try new places all the time, but I guess...
spk_1: I mean, I don't have it often, but there's a place called Owie in Bristol, which is like a burger place that does good vegan burgers and good normal burgers.
spk_1: Yeah, they're very good, but it's not really a go-to.
spk_1: Yeah, no, not really.
spk_1: We try and just have different things, I think.
spk_0: Okay. Would you say your ordering habits have changed over time?
spk_1: What, with regards to takeaways?
spk_0: Yeah, just... I suppose with you obviously just transitioning, as well, out of being a postgraduate student to full-time work.
spk_0: Yeah.
spk_1: I think, really, I probably get a bit more...
spk_1: more takeaways. I think I end up with a few of those different reward schemes, and there seem to be lots of offers.
spk_1: So, yeah, I probably get more over time than I used to.
spk_0: Okay, what about groceries, then?
spk_0: Do you ever get groceries for delivery via one of the apps, like Delivery or Just Eat?
spk_1: Yeah, I do, to be fair.
spk_1: And I didn't used to...
spk_1: Yeah, I've been using that the last few months, a bit more.
spk_1: And again, I use all three of those apps, really, and I found, actually, they're more economical than I thought they would be, provided you go for either deals or dispatches or stuff.
spk_1: But yeah, I'd probably use that maybe once or twice a week...
spk_1: Maybe once a week, probably, on average.
spk_0: Interesting... And what, what sort of items are in those orders?
spk_1: So, sometimes I'll order us something that we can have for dinner, like some pizzas from, I don't know, Co-op or whatever.
spk_1: And then, whilst I'm doing that, get sort of stuff for the house, you know...
spk_1: kind of squash and bread and eggs and milk and household bits and bobs, maybe some cleaning stuff, and I...
spk_1: Yeah, we do that more so on the weekend than the week, I'd say.
spk_1: Yeah. It just depends, really depends what I need.
spk_0: Does it...? Okay, how do you decide whether to go to the shop versus ordering groceries for delivery?
spk_1: Yeah, for me, none of them are particularly walkable, so it's either me driving there or getting them delivered.
spk_1: And it depends on the weather, how I'm feeling, and what we're up to.
spk_1: But in the winter, there's probably more tendency to get it delivered...
spk_1: just that pure laziness.
spk_0: Yeah, fair... It's also dark and cold, so it doesn't help.
spk_1: Yeah, exactly.
spk_0: Okay, so what would you say, then, stops you from ordering groceries for delivery more often?
spk_1: Just because I often will get it on my way back from work, so there's not really a need during the weekdays...
spk_1: I guess if I were to be less organized, do less planning, then maybe I'd get it more often.
spk_1: And on days off or weekends, I would do it more often.
spk_0: And then, how much is price taken into consideration when you...
spk_0: when you're shopping for...
spk_0: for your groceries?
spk_1: I think it's important, but sometimes it doesn't feel super transparent in terms of the costings and pricings of things.
spk_1: It feels like you end up sometimes paying a lot more on the app than you would in the shop, whether that's true or not...
spk_1: I don't know, but it feels that way.
spk_1: So I'm less inclined to do it on the app if there's no sort of discounts, offers, or vouchers.
spk_1: So, you know, paying full price for everything, plus all the deliveries and stuff, is actually quite expensive.
spk_1: So I'm more inclined to do it when there's vouchers, codes, or offers!
spk_0: Absolutely! Have you seen any promos for grocery delivery recently?
spk_1: Yeah, there have been quite a few recently.
spk_1: I'm trying to think of which apps I saw them on...
spk_1: I did one on Co-Op the other day on one of the apps, and it gave me quite a lot off.
spk_1: It was like 20 quid off, and I forget which app it was now, but yeah, there seems to be quite a few!
spk_0: Okay. Did it change, like, how you ordered at all...
spk_0: you know, in terms of quantity, things like that?
spk_1: Yeah, well, I think I saw it on maybe a social media advert for the...
spk_1: the discount, or someone else posted about something, and then that made me actually do the order.
spk_1: And then you had to spend a minimum amount, so I probably would have...
spk_1: probably spent more than I would have, maybe, and did the order that I wasn't going to do, probably.
spk_1: So yeah, so having it definitely influenced my shopping habits!
spk_0: Okay, Brian, thank you.
spk_0: So, focusing then on...
spk_0: on the... the transition from student to...
spk_0: to graduate, did you use delivery apps when you were a student?
spk_1: Yeah, I mean, I guess my student side is slightly different...
spk_1: It's slightly atypical to others.
spk_1: I mean, slightly odd in terms of the first bit of my PhD was unfunded, and I wasn't working much, or...
spk_1: No, it's funded, and I wasn't working much, but I wasn't really getting much of a salary.
spk_1: So I was... I was saving a lot of money then.
spk_1: And then I went back to work for the last year a bit, so it's kind of earning money at that point.
spk_1: So I guess there was that transition period where I went from trying to save money and not spend much to back to normal, and rather than since...
spk_1: Since finishing, if that makes sense.
spk_1: But yeah, so definitely the difference in salary, difference in wage, made a difference in using the different apps!
spk_0: Okay, so you were using them less...
spk_0: as a student?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah.
spk_0: Were you still using all three platforms back then?
spk_1: No, I think I only had Delivery Plus through...
spk_1: through Amazon, I think it was.
spk_1: So I was mainly using that one, and I ended up getting, I think, Uber One through my bank maybe.
spk_1: And then I paid for Just Eat, one with the Just Eat Plus or whatever it is.
spk_1: So I've been using this more since going back to full-time work.
spk_0: Absolutely. Okay...
spk_0: And then, were you aware of the student benefits during your time at uni?
spk_1: I don't think so, no.
spk_1: I think I had Amazon Prime Student and then had the delivery thing through that, but otherwise, no...
spk_1: wasn't aware.
spk_0: Okay, but you were aware of the delivery, student account...
spk_0: and you were aware.
spk_1: I had the Amazon Prime Student.
spk_1: Had delivery through that...
spk_1: I don't know if I had the delivery student account.
spk_1: If that's different, I'm not sure.
spk_0: Yes... so it was Rue plus Silver is free for students.
spk_0: Yeah, had that one, and then Uber One has reduced subscription fees for students.
spk_1: I don't think I knew about that one.
spk_0: Okay.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Can you remember what you would typically order as a student?
spk_1: Often, when I had deadlines coming up and I was focused on studying or doing...
spk_1: doing work, I'd be less organized to go get food.
spk_1: So, I'd be more... more likely to get takeaways.
spk_0: And... was that always on delivery?
spk_1: I think it was... yeah.
spk_1: From memory? Predominantly, yeah.
spk_1: Yeah, I'd say delivery.
spk_0: Okay, great. So, obviously, you were chatting about your behaviors changing since then...
spk_0: You've obviously got all three applications and subscriptions now, as well.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Do you find yourself ordering more now because you have those subscriptions?
spk_1: I think so... I'd say so, yeah.
spk_0: And then, do you have, like, a preference of app?
spk_1: Yes. Based on availability locally, I find delivery the best.
spk_1: It seems to have the best options.
spk_1: It doesn't feel necessarily the cheapest, but it's got the best options.
spk_1: So, probably my go-to first, followed by Just Eat, followed by UberEats...
spk_1: probably just in terms of the quantity and quality of restaurant choices available.
spk_0: So, I'm interested in...
spk_0: Obviously, Delivery was your favorite at uni.
spk_0: It's your favorite now, but you've now got Uber One, and you've got Just Eat.
spk_0: So, what made you, one, use those services, and then, two, subscribe to both of them as well?
spk_1: Yeah, so I think, to be honest, I think I actually got them all free.
spk_1: So, I think I got delivery through Amazon.
spk_1: It's not free—you're paying for Amazon—but through Amazon.
spk_1: And then I think I got Uber through my bank, maybe.
spk_1: And then I think I got Just Eats...
spk_1: got some sort of thing on O2 Priority, which gave me some...
spk_1: I know, one membership or something.
spk_1: So I didn't actually end up paying for them; I just ended up with them.
spk_1: And then... yeah. What was the other part of your question?
spk_0: Yeah, I was... I was interested in why you started using those services, and then why subscribe?
spk_0: So I think that kind of answered both of those questions...
spk_0: sure, in there. So, thank you!
spk_0: Okay, do you still order the same types of meals from the same restaurants as you did when you were at university?
spk_1: I think I would get better and more now...
spk_1: Spend more? Yeah, I think I'll just be inclined to spend a bit more, have slightly nicer stuff or more stuff than one assumes, just because of disposable income.
spk_1: But similar sort of, I guess, genres of food as such.
spk_0: So, what would you say tends to matter most in the moment when you order?
spk_0: I have a list of five things: one, price; number two, offers and promotions; three, speed; four, habit; and number five, subscription benefits.
spk_1: I wouldn't say... four or five.
spk_0: So.
spk_1: Was it price... and what was the second one?
spk_0: Offers and promotions...
spk_0: Yeah, speed, force, habit.
spk_1: Not massive for me because they all seem fairly similar.
spk_1: I think if it's going to be massively skewed really long, then it would put me off, but otherwise, it's not what I'd go to.
spk_1: So I guess out of those options, I'd go for, like, offers and then price as 1 and 2, and then I guess speed three.
spk_1: Habit 5... and I can't remember the other ones.
spk_1: What was number four?
spk_0: "Said? Speed habit...
spk_0: Subscription benefits, offers, and promotions."
spk_1: Subscription benefits, I would put quite low down.
spk_1: Okay, so... yeah, so predominantly kind of offers, promotions, and prices.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. So now, what I want you to do is rate how well Delivery, Just Eat, and Uber Eats provide those on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is extremely terrible and 5 is extremely good.
spk_0: Okay, so firstly, price delivery out of five.
spk_1: I'd say four. Yeah, feels slightly cheaper.
spk_1: So I think just eating...
spk_1: Uber... Uber Eats...
spk_1: our book. No, sorry, Delivery is a 3, and both the other ones are a 4 for price.
spk_0: Okay, so... JustEat and Uber Eats are better than delivery for price?
spk_1: Yeah, I'd say so...
spk_1: Delivery feels slightly more expensive.
spk_0: Okay... what about offers and promotions?
spk_1: I would say they're all pretty much the same on that front...
spk_1: so I'd give them three for all of them.
spk_0: What about speed?
spk_1: I think delivery is probably slightly better...
spk_1: so four for delivery and three for the other two.
spk_0: Habit.
spk_1: And so, by that, you mean my habit to use them, or...?
spk_0: Yeah.
spk_1: I'd say four for delivery, and three for the other two.
spk_0: And... subscription benefits, then?
spk_1: I'm not really sure what the subscription benefits are, as in...
spk_1: what do you get for having a subscription?
spk_0: Yeah, so Uber... Uber ones offer, I think, delivery, and they also offer discounts on taxis and things like that, and then deliveries...
spk_0: free delivery over a certain threshold, etc.
spk_1: Yeah, I think... I think they're all okay.
spk_1: None of them are superb, so I'd give them all three.
